questions.question: Who creates supplier records in the operational master data tool?

expected_answer_facts.text: trading support assistant or master data operator creates the supplier record
aliases: master data operator creates supplier records
trading support assistant creates the supplier record

questions.question: Who owns service rules such as ordering cadence and delivery timing?

expected_answer_facts.text: logistics-side owner defines service rules
aliases: logistics-side owner owns delivery patterns

questions.question: Who controls launch timing and site-level sellability for articles?

expected_answer_facts.text: ranging or assortment owner controls launch timing and site-level sellability
aliases: assortment owner controls launch timing
ranging owner controls site sellability

questions.question: Who governs restricted article-list access?

expected_answer_facts.text: profile or access owner controls restricted lists
aliases: access owner controls who may use restricted lists

questions.question: Who owns packaging-waste reporting requirements?

expected_answer_facts.text: regulatory or reporting owner owns packaging-waste reporting requirements
aliases: reporting owner owns packaging-waste reporting

questions.question: Who creates service items in the system?

expected_answer_facts.text: master data operator creates service items

questions.question: Who keeps day-one packaging design proportionate?

expected_answer_facts.text: architecture owner keeps day-one packaging design proportionate
aliases: architecture owner avoids unnecessary packaging-item complexity

questions.question: Who validates error handling and file-format rules in article upload rollout?

questions.question: Who owns article attributes and imported classifications governance?

aliases: data governance owner reviews imported classifications

questions.question: Which controls must pass before a supplier can move forward?

expected_answer_facts.text: due diligence and credit checks are gating controls
aliases: credit checks are gating controls
due diligence checks must be completed before the supplier can move forward

questions.question: Which contract types must be linked before a supplier can be used downstream?

expected_answer_facts.text: commercial contract, service contract and payment contract are all required
aliases: commercial, service and payment contracts are mandatory
all three contract types must be linked

questions.question: Which systems or process areas are affected by missing supplier contract links?

expected_answer_facts.text: downstream price and assortment modules consume supplier and contract structure
aliases: price and assortment usage depends on supplier contract links
downstream price and assortment modules fail if mandatory contracts are missing

questions.question: Which data affects schedule generation?

expected_answer_facts.text: schedule generation should reflect supplier-side service rules and site-side calendar rules
aliases: supplier service rules and site calendars affect schedules
ordering days and site closures affect schedule generation

questions.question: Which upload checks reject incomplete article data before processing?

expected_answer_facts.text: mandatory-field checks and referential checks reject incomplete or invalid data
aliases: staging validation checks reject incomplete data
format and referential checks must pass before processing

questions.question: Which article data is needed before supplier relationships, pricing and assortments can be built?

expected_answer_facts.text: the article structure must be complete enough before supplier relationships, pricing and assortments can be built
aliases: article master structure must be complete
article setup must be complete before pricing and assortments

questions.question: Which downstream relationship makes active articles different from sellable articles?

expected_answer_facts.text: site sellability depends on later price and assortment associations
aliases: active article does not automatically mean site sellable
assortment and pricing control site sellability

questions.question: Which criteria can automatic article lists use?

expected_answer_facts.text: automatic list criteria can include manufacturer, attributes, hierarchy nodes and other supported fields
aliases: manufacturer, attributes and hierarchy nodes can drive automatic lists

questions.question: Which consumer receives shelf-packaging information?

expected_answer_facts.text: planning or layout consumer receives shelf-packaging information

questions.question: Which mapping still matters for service items even without full stock logic?

expected_answer_facts.text: downstream mapping must remain accurate for special item handling
aliases: special item codes must map correctly into connected systems

questions.question: Why are due diligence and credit checks treated as gating controls in supplier setup?

expected_answer_facts.text: checks must be completed before the supplier can move forward
aliases: supplier cannot proceed if checks fail
credit checks are mandatory gates

questions.question: Explain why supplier status alone is not enough readiness control.

expected_answer_facts.text: mandatory contracts, mapping and readiness controls must also be in place
aliases: active-looking supplier can still fail downstream
status alone is not enough

questions.question: Why does the schedule design need architecture and data-owner agreement?

expected_answer_facts.text: source-of-truth positions and master-and-consumer model must be agreed
aliases: architecture signs off the master-and-consumer model
data ownership must be explicit

questions.question: Why does bulk article upload use a staging area?

expected_answer_facts.text: staging validation catches mandatory-field, format and referential errors before live processing
aliases: errors are corrected in staging before processing
staging protects live article tables

questions.question: Explain the data graveyard risk in article attributes.

expected_answer_facts.text: attributes without a clear owner and use case risk becoming unmanaged or unused
aliases: loading attributes without ownership creates a data graveyard
attributes should only exist with business owner and use case

questions.question: Why is an active article not automatically sellable at site level?

expected_answer_facts.text: site sellability depends on pricing and assortment-related setup
aliases: launch timing and site sellability are controlled later
active article still needs price and assortment associations

questions.question: Why does article-list governance matter?

expected_answer_facts.text: flexible list creation can lead to duplication, confusion or unused lists without an owner
aliases: list sprawl is reduced by governance
lists need a real business use case

questions.question: Why should packaging design avoid overengineering day one?

expected_answer_facts.text: dedicated packaging-item model should only be introduced where movement tracking or returns are needed
aliases: day-one scope should stay pragmatic
avoid complex packaging model without operational requirement

questions.question: Why are service items treated differently from standard stocked articles?

expected_answer_facts.text: service items are non-stock or non-logistics items and do not need normal logistics-unit setup
aliases: service items use simplified non-stock setup
fuel and similar items remain service items for day one

questions.question: Explain why downstream testing is important for tax changes, article publication and product changes.

expected_answer_facts.text: testing validates publication timing, tax updates, product-change behaviour, article-list logic and consumer-system responses end to end
aliases: end-to-end testing validates downstream consumer behaviour
tax and product changes must be tested across systems
